problem.title: One Edit Distance
problem.description: Given two strings s and t, return true if they are both one edit distance apart, otherwise return false. An edit is an insertion, deletion, or substitution of a single character.
assumptions: Return true if s and t are exactly 1 edit distance apart.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Character Comparison [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Walk both strings with two pointers; the moment a mismatch is found, count it as the single allowed edit and advance the pointer(s) appropriately — advance both for a substitution, only the longer-string pointer for an insertion/deletion. If a second mismatch is found, return false. The key insight is handling the three edit types differently based on which string is longer, and counting any leftover character at the end as the final edit.
